rules.title: Issue closed, lane still active
rules.lesson: C36 — cross-tier joins expose contradictions a stateless worker must resolve FIRST; issue-closed-lane-active is the prototype's founding case
rules.rationale: The work surface says in-progress, the tracker says done — one of them lies, and everything derived downstream (leases, orient, the backlog) inherits the lie until a human resolves it. Deterministic (the forge SAID closed); an unknown state is never divergence. Severity warn, tag DIVERGED; exit unchanged until M7.
rules.fix: Resolve the contradiction, not the symptom: merge/close the lane if the work is done, or reopen the issue (gh issue reopen <n>) if it isn't.

rules.title: Recorded next: points at a closed issue
rules.lesson: C06/C36 — the pause-state is only worth resuming from while the world it names still exists; a next: at a closed issue resumes into a wall
rules.rationale: The newest session record's next: names an issue the forge says is closed — the recorded plan is stale, and a successor following it verbatim works on a dead thread. Generalizes the ADR-0009 prototype's next:-pointer check. Deterministic; unknown states are never divergence. Tag DIVERGED; exit unchanged until M7.
rules.fix: Re-derive (baseline orient), then re-log with a live next step: baseline log -m "..." --next "...".

rules.title: An open PR closes an already-closed issue
rules.lesson: C36 — done-with-nothing-merged: the issue closed some other way while the PR that was supposed to close it still floats
rules.rationale: A live PR declaring "closes #N" where #N is already closed means the work either landed twice, landed elsewhere, or was abandoned without saying so — repo-wide, not lane-scoped, because the floating PR poisons the whole backlog view. Deterministic; tag DIVERGED; exit unchanged until M7.
rules.fix: Retarget the PR at a live issue, or close it naming where the work actually landed.
